title: Fix bonkers-search follow-up contract and static-gate regressions
description: Repair post-merge regressions from bonkers-search hardening by documenting chunk_span_integrity_error in docs/ERROR_CODES.md and resolving Ruff/static-gate violations in the new indexer integrity checks, then rerun the failing contract and static-quality suites.
close_reason: Fixed contract fixture, docs catalog entry, and static-gate formatting regressions

title: Fail-loud bonkers search hardening and RCA coverage
description:
This issue landed as a fail-loud search-health contract, not a ranking-only tweak. The current code spreads the fix across three layers: `src/gloggur/cli/main.py` builds a shared health snapshot for `status` and CLI search; `src/gloggur/search/hybrid_search.py` marks `HybridSearch.search()` as legacy and returns blocked or diagnostic payloads when health says search is unsafe; and `src/gloggur/search/router/engine.py` suppresses semantic-only auto-mode results that stay below quality thresholds.

The integrity half of the fix also shipped: `src/gloggur/indexer/cache.py` persists search-integrity markers, `src/gloggur/indexer/indexer.py` validates vector/cache and chunk/span integrity before publication, and `docs/BONKERS_SEARCH_FAILURE_MATRIX.md` documents the concrete failure families. The practical boundary is important: the repo does not promise perfect semantic ranking; it now promises machine-readable suppression and provenance when retrieval is stale, legacy, or below threshold.
design:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`: `_build_search_health_snapshot(...)` is the shared health source for `status`, CLI search, and legacy compatibility payloads.
- `src/gloggur/search/hybrid_search.py`: legacy `HybridSearch.search()` now exposes `entrypoint`, `contract_version`, `warning_codes`, `semantic_search_allowed`, and a deprecation notice instead of looking like a clean modern surface.
- `src/gloggur/search/router/engine.py`: `_route_auto(...)` is where semantic-only below-threshold results are suppressed with `ungrounded_results_suppressed`.
- `src/gloggur/indexer/cache.py` and `src/gloggur/indexer/indexer.py`: persist and validate the `vector_cache` / `chunk_span` integrity markers that feed search suppression.
- `docs/BONKERS_SEARCH_FAILURE_MATRIX.md`, `docs/ERROR_CODES.md`, `tests/unit/test_search_router.py`, `tests/unit/test_hybrid_search.py`, `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py`, and `tests/integration/test_cli.py` are the contract and regression surfaces for this behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- Shared search health is visible in `status --json`, CLI `search --json`, and legacy `HybridSearch.search()` payloads instead of being CLI-only.
- Legacy HybridSearch callers receive explicit legacy diagnostics and blocked payloads when the cache is non-ready or semantic search is disabled.
- Auto router mode suppresses semantic-only below-threshold outcomes with `ungrounded_results_suppressed` instead of returning plausible-looking junk hits.
- Search-integrity markers for `vector_cache` and `chunk_span` are persisted and used to disable semantic retrieval when integrity is failed or missing.
- Docs and tests describe the shipped fail-loud behavior as an evidence/suppression contract, not as a promise that semantic ranking is always correct.
notes:
- `docs/BONKERS_SEARCH_FAILURE_MATRIX.md` now enumerates entrypoint/version skew, index health, vector/cache divergence, chunk/span integrity, routing degradation, model quality, and payload projection as separate failure families.
- `tests/unit/test_search_router.py::test_search_router_suppresses_semantic_only_below_threshold_results` locks the semantic-only suppression path.
- `tests/unit/test_hybrid_search.py::test_semantic_search_blocks_when_health_requires_reindex` and `::test_semantic_search_disabled_returns_blocked_payload_with_explicit_warning` lock the legacy HybridSearch diagnostics.
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_build_status_payload_surfaces_missing_search_integrity_markers` and `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_index_reports_vector_metadata_mismatch_on_tampered_vector_map` cover the integrity-marker and fail-closed index/search surfaces.
- This issue should now be read as the record of the shipped bonkers-search containment contract, not as an open-ended search-quality umbrella.

title: Fix exact mode search returning empty hits in CLI JSON round-trip
description:
The original issue body captured an early blocked `search --json` payload, but the current shipped fix lives in the exact backend rather than in a generic CLI wrapper. `src/gloggur/search/router/backends.py` now falls back to a Python exact scan when ripgrep is unavailable, skips probable binary/cache files such as `.gloggur/index/symbols.db`, and reads text with UTF-8 replacement instead of dying on decode errors. That restores deterministic exact hits and stable byte spans for exact-mode callers while leaving semantic and symbol search behavior unchanged.

The practical outcome is that the end-to-end CLI round-trip in `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_search_extract_round_trip_for_exact_semantic_and_symbol_modes` now works after a successful index, and the router records the fallback path via `python_fallback_exact_scan` instead of silently returning empty hits.
design:
- `src/gloggur/search/router/backends.py`
  - `run_exact_backend(...)` detects ripgrep unavailability and records `python_fallback_exact_scan` when the fallback path is used.
  - `_iter_fallback_search_files(...)` and `_is_probably_binary(...)` keep binary/cache files out of the exact scan.
  - `_run_exact_backend_fallback_scan(...)` reads candidate files with `encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"` and emits normal exact hits with byte offsets.
- Regression anchors:
  - `tests/unit/test_search_exact_backend.py::test_run_exact_backend_falls_back_when_ripgrep_is_unavailable`
  - `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_search_extract_round_trip_for_exact_semantic_and_symbol_modes`
- Scope clarification:
  - this fix hardens the exact backend path only; semantic and symbol modes keep their existing contracts.
acceptance_criteria:
Exact-mode search returns deterministic hits even when ripgrep is unavailable.
The fallback scan does not fail on binary/cache files or Unicode decode problems.
CLI `search -> extract` round-tripping succeeds for exact, semantic, and symbol modes after indexing.
The exact-backend hardening does not regress semantic or symbol search behavior.
notes:
The existing close reason already points at the key fix: exact-backend fallback hardening around Unicode decode failure.
Verification on 2026-03-26 was anchored to `tests/unit/test_search_exact_backend.py::test_run_exact_backend_falls_back_when_ripgrep_is_unavailable`, `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_search_extract_round_trip_for_exact_semantic_and_symbol_modes`, and direct inspection of the fallback helpers in `src/gloggur/search/router/backends.py`.
close_reason: Fixed exact backend fallback to avoid UnicodeDecodeError and restored search CLI integration stability

title: Add byte-range extraction and search span metadata
description: Current code already shipped byte-range-aware search and extraction as a cross-surface contract, not just a new `extract` subcommand. `src/gloggur/cli/main.py` implements `extract <path> <start_byte> <end_byte>` with repo-relative path validation and stable failure codes, and the same module attaches additive `start_byte` / `end_byte` fields to routed search and find JSON hits. `src/gloggur/symbol_index/indexer.py` and `src/gloggur/symbol_index/store.py` persist byte spans for symbol occurrences, while `src/gloggur/search/router/backends.py`, `src/gloggur/search/compat.py`, and `src/gloggur/search/hybrid_search.py` preserve those spans across exact, semantic, symbol, router, and compat result paths. The shipped operator contract is the documented `search -> extract` round trip on repo-relative paths.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
  - `_parse_extract_byte_value(...)`, `_extract_payload(...)`, and `extract` implement the CLI contract and stable failure codes.
  - routed hit serialization includes additive `start_byte` / `end_byte` fields.
- `src/gloggur/byte_spans.py`
  - owns repo-relative path handling and byte-slicing helpers used by extract/search flows.
- `src/gloggur/symbol_index/indexer.py` and `src/gloggur/symbol_index/store.py`
  - persist byte spans for symbol occurrences.
- `src/gloggur/search/router/backends.py`, `src/gloggur/search/compat.py`, and `src/gloggur/search/hybrid_search.py`
  - preserve span metadata across backend and compatibility layers.
- `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`, `docs/SEARCH_JSON_V2_MIGRATION.md`, and `docs/state-diagrams/simple-command-reference.md`
  - document the public JSON and extraction contract.

Scope clarification:
- the public path contract here is repo-relative, even when internal code also manipulates absolute paths;
- byte metadata is additive and intended to compose with existing search payloads.
acceptance_criteria:
- `gloggur extract <path> <start_byte> <end_byte>` works in plain-text and `--json` modes on repo-relative paths.
- Invalid ranges and missing paths fail with stable machine-readable extract error codes.
- Search/find JSON hits carry additive `start_byte` / `end_byte` across exact, semantic, and symbol result paths.
- Search results can be round-tripped into `extract` without path-shape ambiguity.
- Regression coverage pins the repo-relative path contract and byte-span persistence.
notes:
Current evidence on 2026-03-26:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py` still exposes `extract_byte_range_invalid` and related extract failure codes.
- `src/gloggur/search/router/backends.py`, `src/gloggur/search/compat.py`, and `src/gloggur/search/hybrid_search.py` still preserve additive `start_byte` / `end_byte` hit metadata.
Focused verification on 2026-03-26:
- `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_extract_success_in_plain_and_json_modes`
- `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_extract_failure_contracts_in_plain_and_json_modes`
- `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_search_extract_round_trip_for_exact_semantic_and_symbol_modes`
- included in the focused pytest batch -> `20 passed, 3 warnings in 3.94s`

title: Stabilize watch failure-contract integration test on CI 3.13
description:
This issue was a test-harness stabilization around the existing watch ready-state contract, not a change to how `vector_metadata_mismatch` is detected. The flaky path lived in `tests/integration/test_watch_cli_lifecycle_integration.py::test_watch_status_fails_closed_from_last_batch_when_summary_counters_drift`, where the test previously mutated the watched file almost immediately after `watch start --daemon --json` returned. On slower GitHub Actions Python 3.13 runs, the daemon could still be in startup, so the test sometimes began polling for `vector_metadata_mismatch` before the backend had reached a stable ready/running state.

The landed fix waits for a stable runtime state before inducing the failure, then makes the failure assertion fail loud with debugging context. The test now polls `watch status --json` until `running=true` and `status in {"running", "running_with_errors"}`, only then mutates the watched file to trigger the tampered-vector failure path, extends the post-mutation polling window, and captures both the last observed status payload and the watch log in the failure message. That turns a CI timing race into a deterministic contract probe for the watch failure surface.

Code-path context for that probe:
- `src/gloggur/watch/service.py:run_forever(...)` writes startup state with `ready=False` / `status="starting"` and only flips to ready/running after the backend loop is live.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_watch_status_ready(...)` and `_normalize_watch_status(...)` are the status-side contract that distinguish `starting` from stable `running` / `running_with_errors`.
- The issue scope was therefore test readiness hardening against that contract, not a semantic change to watch indexing, cache integrity checks, or failure-code synthesis itself.
design:
- `tests/integration/test_watch_cli_lifecycle_integration.py`
  - in `test_watch_status_fails_closed_from_last_batch_when_summary_counters_drift(...)`, the landed change from commit `2244702` adds a precondition loop that waits for a stable watch status before writing the triggering source edit.
  - the test now stores `running_payload` to prove startup completed, tracks `last_status_payload` during failure polling, increases the mismatch polling budget from 120 to 200 iterations, and includes the watch log content in the final assertion when the expected failure never appears.
- `src/gloggur/watch/service.py`
  - `run_forever(...)` is the runtime behavior the test is waiting on: it first persists `status="starting"`, `ready=False`, and only later writes `ready=True` plus `status="running"` / `"running_with_errors"` once the watcher backend is actually live.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
  - `_watch_status_ready(...)`, `_normalize_watch_status(...)`, and `_create_watch_status_payload(...)` define the status semantics the integration test now uses as its stable-start barrier.
- `docs/state-diagrams/watch-command-state.md`
  - the current docs match this contract explicitly: `starting` is pre-ready, while `running` / `running_with_errors` are ready states.

Important scope boundary:
- this issue is about making the integration test respect the runtime startup contract and emit better diagnostics when that contract is violated or delayed;
- later runtime hardening for fail-closed `last_batch` code/guidance synthesis landed in separate commits (`296d2bf`, `be82c38`) and should not be backfilled into this issue as if they were part of the original CI race fix.
acceptance_criteria:
The watch lifecycle integration test does not begin the failure probe until `watch status --json` reports a stable ready state (`running` or `running_with_errors` while `running=true`).
The tampered-vector failure path still produces `vector_metadata_mismatch` through the watch status contract once the backend is actually running.
When the expected failure does not appear, the test fails loud with the last observed status payload and watch log content instead of a context-free timeout.
The issue remains scoped to test/CI startup-race hardening rather than changing production watch failure semantics.
notes:
Landed in commit `2244702` (`test(watch): harden failure-contract integration startup readiness on CI`).
Current runtime contract referenced by the test lives in `src/gloggur/watch/service.py` and `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`, and the state model is documented in `docs/state-diagrams/watch-command-state.md`.
Later watch failure-contract fixes, especially fail-closed synthesis from `last_batch` payloads, landed separately in `296d2bf` and `be82c38`; this issue should remain documented as the earlier CI/test stabilization pass.
Focused verification on 2026-03-26:
- `./.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/integration/test_watch_cli_lifecycle_integration.py::test_watch_status_fails_closed_from_last_batch_when_summary_counters_drift` -> `1 passed in 7.54s`
close_reason: Implemented watch integration test startup-race hardening and verified with repeated 3.13 runs

title: Preserve watch last_batch failure_codes for vector_metadata_mismatch drift
description:
Current watch status still preserves `last_batch.failure_codes` and `last_batch.failure_guidance` when top-level summary counters drift, instead of dropping the machine-readable cause from the most recent failing batch. `src/gloggur/cli/main.py` still reconstructs the watch failure contract so that `last_batch` reason codes are preferred when they exist, and only falls back to synthetic inconsistency codes like `watch_last_batch_inconsistent` when no real batch-level reason codes survive.

This issue is specifically about preserving the deterministic `vector_metadata_mismatch` failure contract through watch status rollups, not about general watch health. The important current-state boundary is that a drifted summary can still report `running_with_errors` with a meaningful `last_batch` cause, while truly code-less drift synthesizes `watch_last_batch_inconsistent` instead of silently erasing the failure reason.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
  - `_build_watch_failure_contract(...)` / watch-status normalization still prefer real `last_batch.failure_codes` and only synthesize `watch_last_batch_inconsistent` when reason codes are missing.
  - the top-level error-guidance table still includes `watch_last_batch_inconsistent` and `vector_metadata_mismatch` remediation.
- `tests/unit/test_cli_watch.py`
  - pins the drifted-summary path that restores `vector_metadata_mismatch` from `last_batch`.
  - also pins the fallback inconsistency contract when `last_batch` lacks machine-readable codes.
- `tests/integration/test_watch_cli_lifecycle_integration.py`
  - proves the end-to-end lifecycle/status surface preserves `vector_metadata_mismatch` in `last_batch` and can still fail closed after manual drift.
- related runtime state path: `src/gloggur/watch/service.py`
  - still writes/backs up `last_batch` failure payloads used by watch status.
- file history:
  - `2f3158f` (`fix(watch): fail closed when running_with_errors loses reason counters`).

Important scope clarification:
- this issue preserved batch-level failure-code fidelity under watch-status rollups;
- it did not change the underlying vector integrity failure itself.
acceptance_criteria:
When watch summary counters drift but the latest batch has machine-readable failure codes, `watch status --json` still reports those real batch-level codes and guidance.
When the latest batch lacks machine-readable reason codes, `watch status --json` fails closed with `watch_last_batch_inconsistent` instead of pretending the daemon is healthy.
The lifecycle integration surface still preserves `vector_metadata_mismatch` through `last_batch` payloads.
notes:
Landed in commit `2f3158f` (`fix(watch): fail closed when running_with_errors loses reason counters`).
Current evidence on 2026-03-26:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py` still prefers `last_batch.failure_codes` over synthesized inconsistency when batch-level reasons are available.
- `tests/unit/test_cli_watch.py` still covers both the restored `vector_metadata_mismatch` path and the fallback `watch_last_batch_inconsistent` path.
- `tests/integration/test_watch_cli_lifecycle_integration.py::test_watch_status_fails_closed_from_last_batch_when_summary_counters_drift` still proves end-to-end preservation of `last_batch.failure_codes` and guidance.
Focused verification on 2026-03-26:
- `tests/unit/test_cli_watch.py::test_watch_status_json_uses_last_batch_failure_reasons_when_top_level_counters_drift`
- `tests/unit/test_cli_watch.py::test_watch_status_json_synthesizes_last_batch_inconsistent_failure_contract`
- `tests/unit/test_cli_watch.py::test_build_watch_failure_contract_prefers_last_batch_failure_codes_over_inconsistency`
- `tests/integration/test_watch_cli_lifecycle_integration.py::test_watch_status_fails_closed_from_last_batch_when_summary_counters_drift`
- included in the focused pytest batch -> `15 passed, 3 warnings in 19.31s`

title: Fix pre-push regressions from symbol path normalization and static gate failures
description: The current code narrows this issue to a more precise shipped contract than the original bug title suggests. The durable fix is the router/symbol-index path-variant compatibility layer plus the repaired static-quality gate surface that stopped pre-push from failing after symbol-index hardening. `src/gloggur/search/router/backends.py` now normalizes path filters and evaluates both absolute and repo-relative variants via `_normalize_path(...)`, `_path_variants(...)`, and `_prefix_variants(...)`, so routed symbol hits and prefix filters tolerate caller/repo path-shape differences instead of requiring one exact spelling. Missing or corrupt symbol indexes are also downgraded to non-fatal backend errors (`symbol index unavailable`) rather than collapsing routed search. In parallel, `scripts/run_static_quality_gates.py` now has an explicit staged contract for ruff/mypy/black target scope, with tests pinning that only ruff and black include `src/gloggur` while mypy stays on the control-plane subset. The historical `/var` vs `/private/var` symptom is better tracked here as one manifestation of the broader path-variant normalization boundary.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/search/router/backends.py`
  - `_normalize_path(...)`, `_path_variants(...)`, `_prefix_variants(...)`, and `_path_matches_prefix(...)` define the routed path-normalization contract.
  - symbol backend error handling returns `symbol index unavailable: ...` instead of crashing the whole search path.
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py`
  - `test_resolve_search_path_filter_for_routing_normalizes_expected_shapes` pins the caller-facing path-filter normalization boundary.
- `tests/unit/test_search_symbol_backend.py` and `tests/integration/test_cli.py`
  - cover missing/corrupt symbol-index behavior as a structured non-fatal backend error.
- `scripts/run_static_quality_gates.py`, `tests/unit/test_run_static_quality_gates.py`, and `tests/integration/test_run_static_quality_gates_harness.py`
  - define and verify the repaired static-gate target scope.
- `.githooks/pre-push`
  - is only the surfacing mechanism here; the actual regression ownership is in router/runtime and static-gate code, not in the hook itself.

Scope clarification:
- this issue is not best described as a hook bug;
- the hook merely exposed path-normalization and static-gate regressions that now have more precise runtime/test ownership.
acceptance_criteria:
- Routed path filters accept stable absolute and repo-relative path variants without requiring one exact path spelling.
- Missing or corrupt symbol indexes surface as structured non-fatal backend errors (`symbol index unavailable`) instead of collapsing routed search behavior.
- The static-quality gate harness keeps explicit target scope, with `src/gloggur` included in ruff/black and excluded from mypy until runtime-package mypy debt is intentionally addressed.
- Focused regression coverage pins the path-filter normalization and static-gate contract that previously failed pre-push verification.
notes:
Current evidence on 2026-03-26:
- `src/gloggur/search/router/backends.py` still uses path-variant normalization helpers rather than one-path-string equality.
- `scripts/run_static_quality_gates.py` still exposes explicit `GATE_TARGETS` vs `MYPY_TARGETS` scope.
Focused verification on 2026-03-26:
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main_command_families.py::test_resolve_search_path_filter_for_routing_normalizes_expected_shapes`
- `tests/unit/test_run_static_quality_gates.py::test_static_gate_target_scope_keeps_runtime_package_in_ruff_and_black_only`
- `tests/integration/test_run_static_quality_gates_harness.py::test_run_static_quality_gates_harness_succeeds_for_verification_surface`
- `tests/unit/test_search_symbol_backend.py::test_symbol_backend_reports_error_when_symbol_index_missing`
- `tests/unit/test_search_symbol_backend.py::test_symbol_backend_reports_error_when_symbol_index_is_corrupt`
- focused verification totals -> `20 passed, 3 warnings in 3.94s` plus `2 passed in 1.77s`

title: [F9] Always-on Symbol Index and Grep-Compatible Query Interface
description:
Most of the originally requested F9 surface is already in place. The repo now has a local symbol occurrence index at `.gloggur/index/symbols.db`, incremental symbol-index builds during `gloggur index`, symbol-backend participation for code identifier/declaration lookups, and grep-compatible query parsing as a router hint layer with plain-query fallback.

`src/gloggur/cli/main.py:_run_symbol_index(...)` resolves the repo root and attaches a `symbol_index` payload to `index --json`. `src/gloggur/symbol_index/indexer.py` incrementally writes definition/reference occurrences and skips unchanged files by file metadata. `src/gloggur/search/router/query_compat.py` parses `rg ...` / `grep ...` syntax into execution hints, globs, and path filters, and `src/gloggur/search/router/engine.py` merges that parsed state into routing without leaking grep-only flags into the public `SearchIntent` object. `src/gloggur/search/router/backends.py:run_symbol_backend(...)` queries `.gloggur/index/symbols.db` for code identifier/declaration flows and keeps missing/corrupt symbol DBs non-fatal.

The remaining value in this issue is hardening and performance follow-up, not inventing the feature from scratch: preserve the symbol-index lifecycle, keep grep-compatible fallback behavior stable, and avoid drift between parsed query hints, router execution, and symbol-backend behavior.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
  - `_resolve_symbol_index_root(...)` chooses the repo root for `.gloggur/index/symbols.db`.
  - `_run_symbol_index(...)` runs `SymbolIndexer` and exposes additive `symbol_index` payload fields on index commands.
- `src/gloggur/symbol_index/indexer.py`
  - incrementally indexes defs/refs into the SQLite symbol store.
  - skips unchanged files and rewrites changed/deleted occurrence rows deterministically.
- `src/gloggur/symbol_index/store.py`
  - owns the on-disk `.gloggur/index/symbols.db` contract and availability/reset behavior.
- `src/gloggur/search/router/query_compat.py`
  - parses grep-compatible command forms, flags, globs, and path operands into `ParsedQueryCompat`.
  - falls back cleanly with `fallback_used=true` / `parse_error` when command parsing is malformed or incomplete.
- `src/gloggur/search/router/engine.py`
  - calls `parse_query_compat(...)`, merges execution hints/path filters, and keeps fixed-string / quoted-pattern behavior aligned with router hints.
- `src/gloggur/search/router/backends.py`
  - `run_symbol_backend(...)` queries the symbol store for identifier/declaration code queries and keeps missing/corrupt DBs non-fatal.
- Docs/tests that pin the current public contract include `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`, `docs/SEARCH_JSON_V2_MIGRATION.md`, `tests/unit/test_symbol_index.py`, `tests/unit/test_search_router.py`, and `tests/integration/test_cli.py`.

Important scope clarification:
- the symbol index is already always built and always available as an additive search capability when present;
- grep-compatible parsing is a hint/execution layer, not a separate public command mode;
- the main remaining gaps are backend efficiency and edge-case hardening, not basic existence of the symbol DB or query-compat parser.
acceptance_criteria:
- `index --json` continues to publish an additive `symbol_index` payload with the `.gloggur/index/symbols.db` path and truthful incremental counters.
- Repeated indexing skips unchanged files in the symbol index while still updating changed/deleted occurrence rows deterministically.
- `search --json --debug-router` continues to accept representative `rg ...` / `grep ...` query forms and surfaces parsed query metadata through `debug.parsed_query`.
- Grep-compatible flags/path operands map into router execution hints and path filters without leaking grep-only flags into `SearchIntent`.
- Symbol backend participation for code identifier/declaration queries remains non-fatal when the symbol DB is missing or corrupt, and callers receive machine-readable backend error details instead of a hard CLI failure.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_index_builds_symbol_index_and_reports_incremental_counters` proves index builds `.gloggur/index/symbols.db` and exposes additive symbol-index counters.
- `tests/unit/test_symbol_index.py` proves the current store/indexer contract for DB path creation, unchanged-file skipping, changed-file replacement, and schema reset/unavailability boundaries.
- `tests/unit/test_search_router.py::test_query_compat_flags_map_to_execution_hints_without_leaking_into_search_intent` proves grep-compatible flags are translated into execution hints rather than mutating `SearchIntent`.
- `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_search_accepts_grep_compatible_query_syntax` proves representative `rg` / `grep` queries surface `debug.parsed_query.source == "grep_compat"` with non-fallback parsing.
- `tests/integration/test_cli.py` also proves missing/corrupt symbol DB behavior stays additive through router debug errors rather than hard-failing search.
- The issue is therefore no longer a blank-slate feature note; the core symbol-index and grep-compat contracts are shipped, and the remaining work is follow-up hardening/performance.

title: Normalize concurrent status corruption recovery operation contract
description:
Current shipped scope is narrower and more concrete than the original one-line regression note. This issue did not change general cache corruption recovery behavior; it normalized the `status --json` failure contract specifically for concurrent recovery races. When two status calls hit the same corrupted cache at the same time, the cache layer can surface low-level operations such as `execute cache database transaction` or `configure cache database pragmas` while one process is actively repairing or recreating state. The shipped fix preserves the bounded recovery behavior but ensures that the losing caller reports the stable user-facing operation `recover corrupted cache database` instead of leaking one of those lower-level internal operation names.

The resulting contract is: `status` retries once on recognized transient corruption-recovery race errors; if the retry succeeds, the command exits zero with normal status payload semantics; if the retry fails with the same transient family, the command exits non-zero with a structured `io_failure` payload whose `error.operation` is normalized to `recover corrupted cache database`. This keeps concurrent status recovery bounded and deterministic for tests, CI, and callers consuming JSON error contracts.
design:
- `src/gloggur/indexer/cache.py` still emits the underlying low-level operation `execute cache database transaction` when sqlite work fails inside the transaction wrapper, and cache initialization can likewise fail at `configure cache database pragmas`. Those internal operation names are correct for the storage layer but too unstable for the `status` command’s top-level recovery contract.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:status(...)` now wraps `_create_status_payload(...)` in a status-specific retry/remap path:
  - `_is_transient_status_race_error(...)` recognizes the transient corruption-recovery race family by operation plus detail tokens such as `no such table`, `file is not a database`, `database disk image is malformed`, and related corruption markers.
  - `status` retries exactly once when the first `StorageIOError` matches that transient race family.
  - if the retry also fails with the same transient family, `_remap_status_recovery_error(...)` rebuilds the exception with the stable operation `recover corrupted cache database` while preserving category, path, detail, and remediation.
- This is intentionally a `status`-surface fix, not a cross-command rewrite of all storage I/O operations. The cache layer still reports its native operation names internally, and other command families keep their own error contracts.
- The concurrency integration in `tests/integration/test_corruption_recovery_integration.py` proves the behavioral boundary: concurrent status attempts remain bounded, at least one caller may succeed after recovery, and any failing caller reports the normalized operation string rather than the raw transaction label.
- The unit tests in `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py` pin both halves of the contract:
  - retry-once success when a transient race clears,
  - retry-once then remap when the transient race persists.
acceptance_criteria:
- Concurrent `status --json` calls against a corrupted cache remain bounded and do not hang indefinitely.
- `status` retries exactly once when the first failure matches the recognized transient corruption-recovery race family.
- If the retry succeeds, `status` exits zero with the normal payload rather than surfacing an intermediate storage-operation error.
- If the retry fails again with the transient race family, the structured JSON error keeps stable fields and normalizes `error.operation` to `recover corrupted cache database`.
- The low-level cache/storage layer may still use internal operation names such as `execute cache database transaction` or `configure cache database pragmas`, but those names do not leak through the final `status` recovery contract in this race scenario.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `src/gloggur/indexer/cache.py` still wraps transaction-level sqlite failures as `StorageIOError(..., operation="execute cache database transaction", ...)`, confirming that the shipped fix lives above the cache layer rather than replacing internal operation naming globally.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:status(...)` now retries `_create_status_payload(...)` once on `_is_transient_status_race_error(...)` matches and remaps a second transient failure through `_remap_status_recovery_error(...)` to `operation="recover corrupted cache database"`.
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_status_retries_transient_no_such_table_race_once` proves the success-on-retry path.
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_status_remaps_repeated_transient_no_such_table_race` and `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_status_remaps_transient_pragmas_error_during_cache_creation` prove the persistent-failure remap path and the two-call retry bound.
- `tests/integration/test_corruption_recovery_integration.py::test_concurrent_status_recovery_attempts_are_bounded` proves the real concurrent-process contract: elapsed time stays bounded, at least one caller can succeed, and any failing caller emits `error.type == "io_failure"` with `error.operation == "recover corrupted cache database"`.
- Focused verification run on 2026-03-26:
  - `./.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_status_remaps_repeated_transient_no_such_table_race tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_status_remaps_transient_pragmas_error_during_cache_creation tests/integration/test_corruption_recovery_integration.py::test_concurrent_status_recovery_attempts_are_bounded`
  - result: `5 passed in 3.25s`
- Important scope boundary:
  - this issue normalized the top-level `status` recovery contract for one transient corruption-race family
  - it did not remove the underlying cache-layer operation names or change broader corruption-recovery mechanics for every command surface

title: Stabilize run_smoke watch_incremental under pytest-xdist parallel load
description:
Original failure mode: `tests/integration/test_run_smoke_harness.py::test_run_smoke_harness_full_workflow_passes` flaked under `pytest-xdist` parallel load because the `watch_incremental` stage in `scripts/run_smoke.py` used repeated `watch status --json` subprocess calls as its polling loop both before and after the watched file changed. Under parallel CI load, that made the stage spend too much of its budget in repeated CLI startup/JSON parsing, and it could also lose the race against the daemon’s first incremental batch after the file mutation.

The shipped fix in commit `fae9532` hardened the smoke harness itself rather than changing the watch daemon contract. `scripts/run_smoke.py` now reads the runtime watch-state file directly through `_read_watch_state()` for low-latency polling, waits for `running=true` via that state file, then rewrites the watched file through `_write_watch_target_update(...)` and keeps polling the state file until either total `indexed_files` or `last_batch.indexed_files` proves the incremental update landed. The stage also snapshots the last observed payload so timeout failures include actionable state context instead of a generic timeout.

Current behavior is more specific than the original one-line issue: the harness now fails fast if the watch stage reports `error_count > 0` or `last_batch.failed > 0` before the incremental update completes, and it retries the watched-file rewrite at fixed attempts `{40, 120, 220}` to create additional deterministic filesystem events when the first mutation is missed under load. The result is a smoke-stage contract that preserves parallel pytest execution while making `watch_incremental` depend on cheap state-file polling plus explicit failure signals instead of expensive repeated CLI status subprocesses.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `scripts/run_smoke.py`:
  - `_env()` pins `GLOGGUR_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER=test` and watch polling env vars (`WATCHFILES_FORCE_POLLING=1`, `WATCHFILES_POLL_DELAY_MS=50`) so smoke runs stay deterministic/offline.
  - `_read_watch_state()` is the main latency reduction: it polls `runtime/watch_state.json` directly instead of shelling out to `watch status --json` on every attempt.
  - `_write_watch_target_update(...)` centralizes the file rewrite used to trigger incremental watch work and varies the helper return value per revision so repeated writes still produce concrete file changes.
  - `_stage_watch_incremental(...)` now uses three distinct phases: wait for `running=true`, write/update the watched file, then wait for either top-level `indexed_files` or `last_batch.indexed_files` to show progress while failing fast on `error_count` or `last_batch.failed`.
  - timeout failures now include `watch_state_file` and `last_payload` context for diagnosis.
- `tests/integration/test_run_smoke_harness.py`: locks the end-to-end contract that the five smoke stages pass in order and that `watch_incremental` remains part of the standard workflow.
- `tests/unit/test_run_smoke.py`: preserves stage-order / blocked-after-failure harness semantics around the stage runner itself.
- Scope boundary: this issue is about the smoke harness polling strategy and stage determinism under parallel load, not about changing the watch daemon’s own runtime contract or disabling `pytest-xdist`.
- Historical note: `fae9532` also touched pre-push environment detection, but that is separate from this issue’s smoke-harness behavior and should not be conflated with the watch_incremental fix.
acceptance_criteria:
- `scripts/run_smoke.py` keeps `watch_incremental` in the standard smoke stage order and the full smoke harness passes without requiring serial pytest execution.
- The `watch_incremental` stage waits for daemon startup via direct watch-state-file polling instead of repeated `watch status --json` subprocess calls.
- The stage recognizes incremental success from either top-level `indexed_files` or `last_batch.indexed_files` in the watch state payload.
- The stage fails immediately if watch processing reports `error_count > 0` or `last_batch.failed > 0` before the expected incremental update lands.
- Repeated deterministic file rewrites are used when necessary to avoid losing the first file-change race under load.
- Timeout failures include enough watch-state context to debug the harness without reproducing the full CI race blindly.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- Landing provenance:
  - commit `fae9532` (`fix(smoke): stabilize watch incremental harness under parallel pytest and harden pre-push env detection`) introduced the smoke-harness changes for this issue.
  - later commit `15dadd7` preserved the same harness structure while tightening the `last_batch.failed` fail-fast check in `scripts/run_smoke.py`.
- Historical diff check:
  - `git show fae9532^:scripts/run_smoke.py` confirmed the old harness polled `watch status --json` directly in both the startup loop and the incremental-update loop.
  - `git show fae9532:scripts/run_smoke.py` confirmed the new harness added `_read_watch_state()`, `_write_watch_target_update(...)`, direct state-file polling, richer timeout context, and repeated update attempts at `{40, 120, 220}`.
- Current code inspection:
  - `scripts/run_smoke.py:_stage_watch_incremental(...)` reads `runtime/watch_state.json` directly, not the CLI, for the hot polling path.
  - `scripts/run_smoke.py:_env()` forces polling watchfiles mode and a short poll delay.
  - `scripts/run_smoke.py:_stage_watch_incremental(...)` treats both top-level `indexed_files` and `last_batch.indexed_files` as success signals and treats `error_count` / `last_batch.failed` as immediate stage failures.
  - `tests/integration/test_run_smoke_harness.py::test_run_smoke_harness_full_workflow_passes` still asserts the smoke stage order `index -> watch_incremental -> resume_status -> search -> inspect`.
- Focused verification run:
  - `./.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/integration/test_run_smoke_harness.py::test_run_smoke_harness_full_workflow_passes tests/unit/test_run_smoke.py`
  - result: `4 passed in 7.54s`
- Direct probe:
  - `./.venv/bin/python scripts/run_smoke.py --format json` returned `ok: true` with `watch_incremental.status = "passed"`, `watch_incremental.context.indexed_files = 1`, `watch_incremental.context.status = "running"`, and full stage summary `{passed: 5, failed: 0, not_run: 0}` in `5902ms`.
- Behavioral boundary clarified by the current code:
  - the shipped fix does not disable parallel pytest execution or change watch daemon semantics; it makes the smoke harness cheaper and more deterministic by polling the watch state file directly and by surfacing incremental-stage errors/timeouts with better context.

title: Fix pre-push hook to avoid broken .venv/bin/pytest path
description:
This issue is narrower and more concrete than the original one-line symlink-error note. The shipped change is a pre-push hook contract hardening in `.githooks/pre-push`: the repo no longer executes `./.venv/bin/pytest` directly, and instead resolves a usable Python interpreter that can run `-m pytest` before invoking the suite.

Current repo state as of 2026-03-26:
- The pre-push hook now probes interpreters in order:
  - `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest --version`
  - `python -m pytest --version`
  - `python3 -m pytest --version`
- Once a usable interpreter is found, the hook runs `PYTHONPATH="$PYTEST_PYTHONPATH" "$PYTEST_PYTHON_BIN" -m pytest -q` instead of shelling into a potentially broken `./.venv/bin/pytest` entrypoint.
- If none of those interpreters can run pytest, the hook fails loud with a stable remediation message telling the operator to run `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh` or activate a working environment.
- After the pytest phase succeeds, the hook still preserves the Beads pre-push integration by delegating to `bd hooks run pre-push` when `bd` is available.

The practical bug boundary is therefore specific: avoid direct execution of a self-referential or otherwise broken `.venv/bin/pytest` shim while keeping the repo’s pre-push test gate and Beads hook behavior intact. This is not a general test-runner redesign; it is execution-path hardening for the pre-push hook.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `.githooks/pre-push`
  - current hook logic sets `PYTEST_PYTHONPATH="${PYTHONPATH:-.}"`
  - probes for a usable interpreter with `-m pytest --version`
  - runs `-m pytest -q` through the chosen interpreter
  - emits explicit fail-loud remediation if no interpreter+pytest combination is usable
  - still chains into `bd hooks run pre-push` after tests pass
- Historical contrast from the parent of `fae9532`
  - the previous hook body was just:
    - `PYTHONPATH="${PYTHONPATH:-.}" .venv/bin/pytest -q`
  - that old path is the concrete source of the issue description because it depended on the `pytest` shim itself being executable and non-recursive
- Commit provenance
  - `fae9532` (`fix(smoke): stabilize watch incremental harness under parallel pytest and harden pre-push env detection`) is the relevant hook-hardening change in git history
- Coverage boundary
  - there does not appear to be a dedicated `pre-push` hook regression test file under `tests/` today
  - current confidence for this issue therefore comes from the live hook contents, shell validation, git history, and repeated successful pre-push executions rather than from a focused hook test module

Important scope boundary:
- this issue is about hook interpreter resolution only
- it does not change which test suite runs, nor the downstream Beads hook integration
- the hook intentionally prefers the repo venv interpreter when available, rather than treating system `python` or `python3` as equivalent first-choice paths
acceptance_criteria:
- `.githooks/pre-push` no longer invokes `./.venv/bin/pytest` directly.
- The hook prefers `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` and only falls back to `python` or `python3` when those interpreters can actually import pytest.
- If no usable interpreter can run pytest, the hook exits non-zero with explicit remediation instead of failing with an opaque shell/symlink error.
- The hook preserves `PYTHONPATH` handling for module execution and still delegates to `bd hooks run pre-push` after the test phase succeeds.
- The fix remains scoped to execution-path hardening; it does not silently weaken or skip the pre-push test gate.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered on 2026-03-26:
- Current hook contents in `.githooks/pre-push` show the interpreter-probing contract:
  - prefer `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest --version`
  - fall back to `python -m pytest --version`
  - then `python3 -m pytest --version`
  - run tests with `"$PYTEST_PYTHON_BIN" -m pytest -q`
- Historical git evidence:
  - `git show fae9532^:.githooks/pre-push` shows the old direct invocation path:
    - `PYTHONPATH="${PYTHONPATH:-.}" .venv/bin/pytest -q`
  - `git show --oneline --no-patch fae9532` identifies the hardening change as:
    - `fae9532 fix(smoke): stabilize watch incremental harness under parallel pytest and harden pre-push env detection`
- Direct local probes on 2026-03-26:
  - `sh -n .githooks/pre-push` -> `OK`
  - `PYTHONPATH="${PYTHONPATH:-.}" ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest --version` -> `pytest 9.0.2`
  - `python -m pytest --version` -> `command not found`
  - `python3 -m pytest --version` -> `No module named pytest`
  - these probes confirm the current environment would select `./.venv/bin/python` and avoid the broken direct `./.venv/bin/pytest` path entirely
- Repo search evidence:
  - `rg` found the current interpreter-based hook logic in `.githooks/pre-push`
  - there is no dedicated `pre-push` hook regression test file under `tests/` today
- Gap between the original low-signal wording and current shipped behavior:
  - the real shipped contract is interpreter resolution plus fail-loud remediation, not merely “use PATH instead”
  - the hook now has an explicit selection order and preserves Beads chaining after tests succeed

title: Rebaseline 3.13 CI performance benchmark after chunk-first rollout
description:
Current checked-in benchmark data already reflects the chunk-first / graph-retrieval envelope. `benchmarks/performance_baseline.json` still stores the generated-fixture phase-4 metrics (`cold_index_duration`, `unchanged_incremental_duration`, `search_average_latency`, and `index_throughput`), `scripts/run_edge_bench.py` still reads that baseline and fails with `performance_threshold_exceeded` when metrics drift beyond policy, and `.github/workflows/performance.yml` still runs the Python 3.13 performance lane against that baseline.

This issue should therefore be recorded as a baseline refresh only, not as a benchmark harness redesign. The benchmark contract stayed the same; the checked-in numbers had to move forward after the chunk-first rollout changed the expected envelope. The important current-state detail is that the baseline file, harness, and CI workflow are still wired together exactly around that refreshed benchmark contract.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `benchmarks/performance_baseline.json`
  - remains the checked-in source of truth for the performance regression envelope.
- `scripts/run_edge_bench.py`
  - still loads the baseline file, applies the regression contract, and emits `performance_threshold_exceeded` when warnings are present.
- `tests/integration/test_run_edge_bench_harness.py`
  - pins baseline writing/reuse and explicit threshold-failure behavior.
- `.github/workflows/performance.yml`
  - still runs on Python 3.13 and executes `python scripts/run_edge_bench.py --benchmark-only --baseline-file benchmarks/performance_baseline.json --format json` in the hosted lane.
- `tests/unit/test_performance_workflow.py`
  - pins the workflow trigger policy, 3.13 benchmark command, artifact upload, and summary contract.
- file history:
  - `3b23f4d` (`ci(benchmark): rebaseline 3.13 performance gate after chunk-first rollout`)
  - context from the rollout itself remains visible in `ab24e51` / `f0ad0b8`.

Important scope clarification:
- this issue updated the checked-in performance envelope;
- it did not replace the benchmark contract, failure code, or hosted workflow shape.
acceptance_criteria:
The checked-in performance baseline reflects the expected post-chunk-first benchmark envelope.
`run_edge_bench.py` continues to compare current metrics against that baseline and fail with `performance_threshold_exceeded` on real regression.
The hosted performance workflow continues to execute the same baseline-backed benchmark command on Python 3.13.
Baseline writing/reuse and threshold-failure behavior remain covered by the harness tests.
notes:
Landed in commit `3b23f4d` (`ci(benchmark): rebaseline 3.13 performance gate after chunk-first rollout`).
Current evidence on 2026-03-26:
- `benchmarks/performance_baseline.json` still contains the refreshed generated-fixture metrics.
- `scripts/run_edge_bench.py` still emits `performance_threshold_exceeded` when the baseline contract returns warnings.
- `.github/workflows/performance.yml` still runs the Python 3.13 benchmark lane against the checked-in baseline.
Focused verification on 2026-03-26:
- `tests/unit/test_performance_workflow.py`
- `tests/integration/test_run_edge_bench_harness.py::test_run_edge_bench_writes_and_reuses_baseline_file`
- `tests/integration/test_run_edge_bench_harness.py::test_run_edge_bench_reports_threshold_failure_code`
- included in the focused pytest batch -> `29 passed, 3 warnings in 40.24s`

title: Rebase ContextPack v2 router branch onto main chunk/graph hard-cut
description: Rebase detached search ContextPack v2 work onto latest origin/main, resolve conflicts with main-first policy, preserve graph commands plus search v2 contract, and return green quickstart/static-gate/full pytest.

title: Fix post-cutover quickstart contract and static gate regressions
description: Resolve failing quickstart contract and run_static_quality_gates harness after search ContextPack v2 cutover by updating docs and lint/format touched files while preserving failure-code contracts.

title: Canonical search ContextPack v2 cutover with contract preservation
description: Current code shows this issue was the hard public cutover of `gloggur search --json` to ContextPack v2, while leaving the older `HybridSearch.search()` surface explicitly marked as legacy instead of silently mutating it. The CLI now emits `schema_version = 2` with `summary` and `hits[]`, removes top-level `results` / `metadata`, carries repo-relative hit paths and byte offsets, and fails closed on removed v1-only grounding/evidence flags with `search_contract_v1_removed`. At the same time, the legacy `HybridSearch.search()` path still returns `results` / `metadata`, but now labels itself with `contract_version = "legacy"`, `entrypoint`, warning codes, and a deprecation notice so older callers cannot mistake it for the authoritative CLI contract. This issue is therefore the search-surface contract migration and compatibility boundary, not just a router refactor.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
  - `_build_search_success_payload(...)` serializes routed search into ContextPack v2.
  - the `search` command hard-codes `contract_version = "contextpack_v2"`.
  - removed v1 grounding/evidence flags now fail through `search_contract_v1_removed`.
- `src/gloggur/search/hybrid_search.py`
  - keeps the old `results` / `metadata` structure for transitional callers, but annotates it with `contract_version = "legacy"`, `legacy_search_contract`, `legacy_search_surface`, and a deprecation notice.
- `docs/SEARCH_JSON_V2_MIGRATION.md` and `docs/QUICKSTART.md`
  - publish the v2 payload shape, removed keys, field mapping, and removed flags.
- `tests/integration/test_cli.py` and `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py`
  - pin the v2 payload shape, removed-key absence, fail-closed legacy flag behavior, and the separation from `find_v1`.

Scope clarification:
- this cutover is about the public JSON contract for `search`;
- it intentionally preserves a labeled legacy compatibility surface for non-CLI callers instead of pretending the old API disappeared overnight.
acceptance_criteria:
- `gloggur search --json` emits ContextPack v2 with `schema_version = 2`, `summary`, and `hits[]`, and does not emit top-level `results` or `metadata`.
- Hit payloads remain repo-relative and byte-addressable so they can round-trip into extract-style tooling.
- Deprecated v1-only grounding/evidence flags fail closed with `search_contract_v1_removed`.
- `HybridSearch.search()` remains clearly labeled as a legacy surface with warning metadata and deprecation notice rather than silently masquerading as ContextPack v2.
- Docs and regression coverage reflect the final CLI-v2 / legacy-surface boundary.
notes:
Current evidence on 2026-03-26:
- `docs/SEARCH_JSON_V2_MIGRATION.md` still documents ContextPack v2 as a hard break and lists removed top-level keys plus removed v1-only flags.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py` still emits `contract_version = "contextpack_v2"` for `search`, while `src/gloggur/search/hybrid_search.py` still exposes a warning-labeled legacy contract.
Focused verification on 2026-03-26:
- `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_search_json_emits_contextpack_v2_without_legacy_keys`
- `tests/integration/test_cli.py::test_cli_search_rejects_legacy_grounding_contract_flags`
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_search_json_contract_remains_contextpack_v2_after_find_addition`
- included in the focused pytest batch -> `20 passed in 3.79s`
close_reason: Implemented ContextPack v2 router cutover, migrated contracts/tests/harnesses, and updated docs.

title: Fix search file filter, profile aliasing, and context radius
description: Current code shows this landed as three tightly related compatibility/UX fixes rather than one generic bug bucket. First, embedding-profile comparison now canonicalizes local/test HuggingFace cache snapshot paths to stable `org/repo` model ids before resume checks and profile drift decisions, so cache metadata written with paths like `.../models--microsoft--codebert-base/snapshots/<sha>` no longer triggers false reindex churn against `local:microsoft/codebert-base`. Second, file scoping on legacy/Hybrid search remains an exact-or-prefix contract rather than naive substring matching: the file filter metadata still reports `file_filter_match_mode = "exact_or_prefix"`, `src` matches `src/...` but not `src2/...`, and unmatched scopes emit `file_filter_no_match` instead of silently broadening the search. Third, search context radius is now a stable operator-facing parameter: the CLI accepts `--context-radius`, search metadata reflects the requested value, and contextual snippets expand accordingly without changing the router contract shape. The real issue is therefore the shipped compatibility surface around profile aliasing, exact+prefix file filtering, and explicit context-window control, not a single search-ranking change.
design:
Implementation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
  - `_canonicalize_hf_snapshot_model(...)`, `_canonicalize_embedding_profile(...)`, `_normalized_cached_embedding_profile(...)`, and `_profile_reindex_reason(...)` define the profile-alias compatibility rules used for resume/reindex checks.
  - `_build_search_health_snapshot(...)` normalizes cached vs expected embedding profiles before health/resume contracts are built.
  - search command wiring still threads `context_radius` through the command surface and health/summary projection.
- `src/gloggur/search/hybrid_search.py`
  - still publishes `_FILE_FILTER_MATCH_MODE = "exact_or_prefix"` and emits file-filter metadata plus warning codes on legacy search surfaces.
- `tests/unit/test_cli_main.py` and `tests/integration/test_cli.py`
  - pin HuggingFace snapshot alias compatibility, context-radius reflection, and exact+prefix file-filter boundaries.
- `tests/integration/test_resume_contract_integration.py`
  - proves the profile alias rule is respected by the resume contract end to end.

Scope clarification:
- this is not the ContextPack v2 cutover itself;
- it is the compatibility hardening that kept profile checks, file scoping, and context windows deterministic across both CLI and resume paths.
acceptance_criteria:
- HuggingFace cache/snapshot model paths and their short `org/repo` aliases compare as resume-compatible when they refer to the same local/test model.
- False profile-drift reindex decisions are eliminated for those canonical alias pairs while non-HF arbitrary local paths remain strict.
- `--file` filtering keeps exact-or-prefix semantics with boundary safety (`src` matches `src/...` but not `src2/...`) and emits `file_filter_no_match` when a requested scope has no hits.
- `--context-radius` remains operator-visible in search output and changes the emitted context window without breaking the surrounding contract.
- Regression coverage pins the profile aliasing, file-filter boundary, and context-radius behavior together.
notes:
Current evidence on 2026-03-26:
