title: Investigate Beads 0.55.4 panic on large imported task descriptions
description:
This issue is best understood as a repo-local Beads stability contract, not a generic “large descriptions crash Beads” report. The current code and docs show that the durable mitigation is: keep one active embedded tracker under `.beads/`, keep the tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl` export in parity with the live DB, and run `bd` commands serially in this repo. The active enforcement surface now lives in `.githooks/pre-commit`, `scripts/check_beads_clone_contract.py`, `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh`, `AGENTS.md`, and `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`.

The historical duplicate embedded repo under `.beads/dolt/beads_gloggur` was quarantined during rollout, but the important long-lived outcome is operational policy. Fresh-clone recovery is driven by `.beads/clone-contract.json` plus bootstrap/import, while normal tracker refresh is driven by `bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl` and `bd hooks run ...` rather than the older rollout-era `bd sync` examples that no longer match the installed CLI.
design:
Current implementation touchpoints:
- `.githooks/pre-commit`: refreshes `.beads/issues.jsonl` with `bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl`, compares dirty state before/after export, and only auto-stages `.beads/issues.jsonl` / `.beads/interactions.jsonl` if the export changed expected tracker files.
- `.githooks/pre-push`, `.githooks/post-merge`, and `.githooks/post-checkout`: delegate to `bd hooks run ...` while preserving the repo’s existing hook ownership under `.githooks`.
- `scripts/check_beads_clone_contract.py`: validates `.beads/config.yaml`, `.beads/clone-contract.json`, `.beads/.gitignore`, and live `bd export` parity with explicit `beads_clone_contract_*` failure codes.
- `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh`: treats fresh clones as `bootstrap_required`, repairs the local Beads DB from tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl`, then verifies imported issue count against the tracked JSONL.
- `AGENTS.md` and `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`: carry the real mitigation that operators must run `bd` commands serially in this repo.

If upstream Beads/Dolt concurrency behavior is ever fixed, this record should only be retired after removing the serialized-usage warning from docs and re-verifying the affected command matrix under parallel access. Until then, the repo contract is deliberately fail-closed around serial usage and export parity.
acceptance_criteria:
- The repo docs explicitly require serialized `bd` usage and do not imply that parallel tracker access is safe here.
- `.githooks/pre-commit` refreshes `.beads/issues.jsonl` via `bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl` and fails loud if the export dirties unexpected paths.
- `scripts/check_beads_clone_contract.py` verifies the clone-contract files plus live export parity and surfaces dedicated `beads_clone_contract_*` failure codes on mismatch/error.
- `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh` bootstraps fresh clones from tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl` and verifies imported count against the tracked export.
- The repo’s current operating contract no longer depends on `bd sync`; the tracked/exported flow is described in terms of `bd export` and hook/bootstrap integration instead.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `AGENTS.md` requires `TODOs.md` / `DONEs.md` coexistence, `.beads/issues.jsonl` parity, and serialized `bd` usage (`run \`bd\` commands serially in this repo`).
- `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md` repeats the serial-use rule and documents fresh-clone bootstrap through `.beads/clone-contract.json` plus `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh`.
- `.githooks/pre-commit` currently contains the active refresh path:
  - `bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl`
  - allowlisted auto-staging for `.beads/issues.jsonl` and `.beads/interactions.jsonl`
  - fail-loud behavior when export dirties unexpected files
- `scripts/check_beads_clone_contract.py` compares tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl` against a live `bd export` into a temp path and fails with `beads_clone_contract_parity_mismatch` or `beads_clone_contract_parity_check_failed` when drift/error is detected.
- The installed `bd` still does not provide `sync`; direct probe returns `Error: unknown command "sync" for "bd"`. The old rollout description therefore understates the current exported/clone-contract enforcement surface.
- The historical quarantine path `.beads_gloggur.quarantine` is still part of the rollout evidence chain, but it is not the ongoing mitigation. The ongoing mitigation is serialized access plus parity verification.

title: Audit TODOs.md for premature DONE promotion
description:
This issue is really a historical backlog-quality record, not a runtime code task. The concrete current-state contract lives in `TODOs.md`, `DONEs.md`, and `docs/verification_artifacts/README.md`: unfinished work stays in `TODOs.md`; completed-but-not-yet-accepted work remains `ready_for_review` with a terminal `DONE Candidate (...)` block; blocked items must include explicit `Unblock Conditions`; only accepted items with verification/evidence move to `DONEs.md`; and durable external proof belongs under `docs/verification_artifacts/` instead of being implied or omitted.

The practical goal of the audit was to stop premature promotion of work into `DONEs.md` just because implementation was complete locally. The normalization pass in commit `b28aafa` converted the Markdown workflow into an evidence-first contract and left existing historical entries in `TODOs.md` until they were actually accepted or their remaining external evidence was called out explicitly.
design:
Current implementation/policy touchpoints:
- `TODOs.md`: now encodes `ready_for_review`, `DONE Candidate`, `Remaining External Evidence`, and `Unblock Conditions` directly in the usage rules and task template.
- `DONEs.md`: defines the immutable completed-work log and requires completion date, delivered scope, verification commands/results, evidence, and follow-ups.
- `docs/verification_artifacts/README.md`: defines the durable repo-tracked landing zone for hosted/live-provider/published-release proof that is not yet in the repo.
- Commit `b28aafa` is the normalization pass that inserted the stronger TODO workflow rules and converted multiple in-progress items into evidence-backed `ready_for_review` records instead of prematurely moving them to `DONEs.md`.

When this historical Markdown workflow is audited again, the main question should be whether an item’s verification commands, evidence paths, and external-proof expectations are explicit enough to justify promotion, not whether code happened to land.
acceptance_criteria:
- `TODOs.md` requires completed-but-not-yet-accepted work to remain `ready_for_review` with a terminal `DONE Candidate (...)` section.
- Blocked Markdown items include exact `Unblock Conditions` rather than vague “waiting on CI” notes.
- `DONEs.md` remains reserved for accepted work with verification and evidence recorded.
- External hosted/live-provider/published-release proof is routed to `docs/verification_artifacts/README.md` instead of being silently omitted during promotion.
- Historical Markdown items are not promoted to `DONEs.md` solely because implementation landed; they need evidence and acceptance.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `TODOs.md` rules now explicitly say:
  - completed-but-not-yet-moved items stay `ready_for_review`
  - blocked items must include `Unblock Conditions`
  - accepted work moves to `DONEs.md`
- `TODOs.md` task template includes `DONE Candidate`, `Verification`, `Evidence`, `Remaining External Evidence`, and `Unblock Conditions` sections.
- `DONEs.md` calls itself the immutable completed-work log and requires evidence plus verification commands/results for moved entries.
- `docs/verification_artifacts/README.md` currently states that no live-provider or published-release upgrade artifacts are checked in yet and explicitly keeps `bd-uhf` and `bd-n1f` blocked until those durable artifacts land.
- `git show b28aafa -- TODOs.md` shows the normalization pass that inserted the new TODO rules/template and converted several rollout items into `ready_for_review` records with explicit remaining external evidence instead of silently promoting them.

title: Evaluate retirement plan for TODOs.md/DONEs.md after Beads rollout
description:
This task is now a concrete coexistence-policy record, not a generic reminder to someday delete `TODOs.md` and `DONEs.md`. The repo currently treats Beads as the canonical tracker for net-new work, while `TODOs.md` and `DONEs.md` remain as historical backlog and verification artifacts. The operative policy lives in `AGENTS.md` and `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`; fresh-clone readiness is defined separately by `.beads/clone-contract.json` and `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh`.

The important current-state nuance is that the historical Markdown files still exist and still contain legacy workflow wording, including `TODOs.md`’s top-level “source of truth” header. Agents are expected to follow the Beads rollout guidance in `AGENTS.md` and `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`, while retaining Markdown history until migration/retirement gates are actually met.
design:
Current policy touchpoints:
- `AGENTS.md`: defines the coexistence contract for net-new work, the retirement preconditions, and the rollback rule if Beads sync/export reliability regresses.
- `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`: mirrors the same transition policy for agent consumers and pairs it with the fresh-clone bootstrap contract.
- `docs/QUICKSTART.md` and `.beads/clone-contract.json`: show that fresh clones still depend on tracked Beads export plus bootstrap/import, which is orthogonal to keeping historical Markdown files in git.
- `docs/verification_artifacts/README.md`: gives blocked or externally-evidenced work a durable repo-tracked landing zone instead of forcing that evidence into ad hoc Markdown notes.

Future retirement work should not be framed as simple file deletion. It needs an explicit archival decision for `DONEs.md`, a resolution of the residual legacy wording in `TODOs.md`, and proof that historical open Markdown tasks are either represented in Beads or intentionally archived with provenance.
acceptance_criteria:
- `AGENTS.md` and `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md` both say that new work goes into Beads while `TODOs.md` / `DONEs.md` remain as historical backlog and verification record during coexistence.
- Markdown retirement is gated on historical open-task migration/archive with provenance, one release cycle with no new Markdown-only work, stable hook integration, and `.beads/issues.jsonl` parity with the live Beads DB.
- The rollback rule is explicit: if Beads export/import reliability regresses, keep Markdown tracking active instead of forcing cutover.
- Fresh-clone bootstrap and clone readiness remain documented through `.beads/clone-contract.json`, `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh`, and `docs/QUICKSTART.md`, rather than being inferred from Markdown tracker presence.
- `TODOs.md` and `DONEs.md` remain tracked in git in this pass; this issue does not itself retire them.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `AGENTS.md` lines 17-25 and 73-84 encode the active coexistence rule: keep `TODOs.md` / `DONEs.md` during the transition, track net-new work in Beads, require `.beads/issues.jsonl` parity, and reopen the retirement decision if Beads export reliability regresses.
- `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md` lines 91-109 repeat the same policy and pair it with `bootstrap_required` clone behavior.
- `docs/QUICKSTART.md` documents that fresh clones ship tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl` and `.beads/clone-contract.json`, not the local `.beads/dolt` database, which reinforces that Markdown retention is a history/archive decision rather than a clone-readiness mechanism.
- `docs/verification_artifacts/README.md` is live and currently says no durable provider/release artifacts are checked in yet; `bd-uhf` and `bd-n1f` remain blocked until those artifacts land there.
- `TODOs.md` still starts with the historical rule `TODOs.md is the source of truth for work that is not finished yet.` That is legacy backlog wording, not the current net-new work policy for agents after the Beads rollout.

title: Beads setup verification task
description:
This issue now maps to a concrete Beads setup verification contract for this repo. A healthy clone needs tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl`, `.beads/clone-contract.json`, `.beads/config.yaml`, and `.beads/.gitignore`; `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh` must be able to bootstrap the local Beads DB from tracked JSONL when `bd status --json` reports that no local database exists; `.githooks/pre-commit` must refresh the tracked export; and `scripts/check_beads_clone_contract.py` must validate that the tracked clone-contract files still match the live export behavior.

The original rollout-era verification text centered on `bd sync --json`, but the current repo contract is more specific and more durable: bootstrap/import for fresh clones, `bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl` for tracked export refresh, `bd hooks run ...` for hook integration, and explicit clone-contract parity checks in code and tests.
design:
Current verification touchpoints:
- `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh`: `ensure_beads_are_ready()` detects the fresh-clone `no beads database found` state, runs `bd init -p bd --json`, imports tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl`, then verifies `bd count --json` against the tracked line count.
- `.beads/clone-contract.json`: publishes `mode = bootstrap_required`, `backend = dolt`, and the exact bootstrap/read commands for machine consumers.
- `.githooks/pre-commit`: keeps tracker export refresh local to the tracked JSONL and preserves existing hook ownership while delegating to `bd hooks run pre-commit`.
- `scripts/check_beads_clone_contract.py`: verifies the fresh-clone contract files and live export parity in a repo-local, fail-loud way.
- `tests/unit/test_check_beads_clone_contract.py`, `tests/integration/test_bootstrap_env_script.py`, and `tests/integration/test_pre_commit_hook.py`: provide the current regression net for clone-contract parity, bootstrap, and hook-export behavior.

Any future rewrite of the setup verification story should update these concrete surfaces together; otherwise the tracker description will drift away from the real bootstrap and hook behavior again.
acceptance_criteria:
- `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh` can bootstrap a fresh clone from tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl` and fails loud on missing `bd`, failed import, or imported-count mismatch.
- `.beads/clone-contract.json` and `.beads/.gitignore` continue to match the contract enforced by `scripts/check_beads_clone_contract.py`.
- `.githooks/pre-commit` refreshes `.beads/issues.jsonl` through `bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl` and only auto-stages the allowlisted tracker files.
- Repo docs continue to state that `bd` commands must run serially here.
- Verification language for this issue describes the current export/bootstrap contract and does not depend on the obsolete `bd sync` command surface.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `tests/unit/test_check_beads_clone_contract.py` covers:
  - parity skipped when `bd` is missing
  - parity verified when `bd export` matches tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl`
  - contract-file validation failures
  - live-export mismatch failures
  - non-clone `bd export` errors
- `tests/integration/test_bootstrap_env_script.py` includes explicit Beads bootstrap cases:
  - `test_bootstrap_bootstraps_beads_from_tracked_jsonl_on_fresh_clone`
  - `test_bootstrap_skips_beads_bootstrap_when_bd_status_is_already_ready`
  - `test_bootstrap_fails_loudly_when_bd_is_missing_for_beads_enabled_repo`
  - `test_bootstrap_fails_loudly_when_beads_import_fails`
  - `test_bootstrap_fails_loudly_when_imported_beads_count_mismatches_jsonl`
- `tests/integration/test_pre_commit_hook.py::test_pre_commit_hook_allows_metadata_deletion_while_refreshing_beads_exports` locks the current allowlisted export-refresh behavior for `.beads/issues.jsonl` and staged metadata deletion.
- The installed `bd` still reports `unknown command "sync"`, so setup verification has effectively moved from `bd sync` toward `bd export`, hook integration, and clone-contract checks.

title: [R10] Performance Benchmarking and Regression Tracking
description:
This issue is no longer a vague future benchmarking idea. Current code already ships a deterministic performance lane: `scripts/run_edge_bench.py` builds a generated fixture corpus by default, measures cold index time, search latency, and unchanged incremental re-index time, compares current metrics against `benchmarks/performance_baseline.json`, and emits a machine-readable `performance_threshold_exceeded` failure when drift exceeds policy. `.github/workflows/performance.yml` runs that harness on Python 3.13 with `GLOGGUR_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER=test`, keeps the lane non-blocking, uploads artifacts, and publishes a job summary.

The remaining open work is therefore not “create a benchmark harness”. The remaining questions are baseline maintenance and performance evidence policy: when to refresh `benchmarks/performance_baseline.json`, how to interpret intentional regressions, and how much hosted artifact/trend history is required before this umbrella can be considered fully retired.
design:
Current implementation touchpoints:
- `scripts/run_edge_bench.py`:
  - `BENCHMARK_THRESHOLD_POLICY` defines the current regression thresholds.
  - `_make_benchmark_fixture(...)` creates the deterministic generated fixture repo used by benchmark-only mode.
  - `_apply_baseline_contract(...)` builds the comparison/threshold/warning payload.
  - benchmark-only mode emits a stable JSON `benchmark_contract` plus a top-level failure when thresholds are exceeded.
- `benchmarks/performance_baseline.json`: checked-in comparison baseline used by local and CI runs.
- `.github/workflows/performance.yml`: runs the benchmark lane on Python 3.13, sets `GLOGGUR_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER=test`, keeps benchmark/pytest steps non-blocking, uploads artifacts, and publishes a summary.
- `docs/VERIFICATION.md`: documents local benchmark invocation and baseline policy.
- `tests/unit/test_run_edge_bench.py`, `tests/integration/test_run_edge_bench_harness.py`, and `tests/unit/test_performance_workflow.py`: pin the local harness and workflow contract.

The remaining scope after the shipped harness is baseline refresh/tuning and hosted evidence, not missing benchmark infrastructure.
acceptance_criteria:
- The benchmark harness runs locally with deterministic JSON output and compares results against `benchmarks/performance_baseline.json`.
- Threshold policy is explicit and machine-readable through the emitted `benchmark_contract` and `performance_threshold_exceeded` failure surface.
- The performance workflow owns the non-blocking benchmark lane, artifact upload, and summary publication on Python 3.13.
- Remaining open work is framed as baseline/evidence maintenance rather than absence of a harness.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- Direct probe: `./.venv/bin/python scripts/run_edge_bench.py --benchmark-only --baseline-file benchmarks/performance_baseline.json --format json` returned top-level `failure.code="performance_threshold_exceeded"` while still reporting a passing phase summary for the three benchmark tests. Current drift warnings were:
  - `cold_index_duration` 2096ms vs threshold 2036.4ms
  - `unchanged_incremental_duration` 973ms vs threshold 450ms
- `tests/integration/test_run_edge_bench_harness.py` currently covers:
  - `test_run_edge_bench_benchmark_only_passes_on_generated_fixture`
  - `test_run_edge_bench_writes_and_reuses_baseline_file`
  - `test_run_edge_bench_reports_threshold_failure_code`
- `tests/unit/test_performance_workflow.py` covers the workflow contract, including non-blocking benchmark/pytest execution, artifact upload, and summary publication.
- Historical landing context: commit `b28aafa` introduced the baseline-backed benchmark gate and docs.

title: [R5] Deterministic New-Session Bootstrap for Local Codex Worktrees
description:
This issue is now best understood as the shipped startup-readiness contract for local worktrees. Current bootstrap does not try to prove the repo is fully indexed; it proves the local environment is coherent enough to start working: `scripts/gloggur status --json` must return a parseable status payload, `scripts/gloggur watch status --json` must return a non-contradictory runtime payload, and the wrapper/bootstrap remediation path must point at one canonical readiness check. The single probe is `scripts/check_startup_readiness.py`.

The important current boundary is that startup readiness is about environment honesty, not semantic-search warmth. A probe can succeed while `needs_reindex=true` and `resume_decision=reindex_required`, as long as those fields are present and the watch state is consistent. This issue is therefore not about fixing watch-mode correctness or auto-starting background services; it is about fail-loud startup diagnostics and one stable command for operators and wrappers.
design:
Current implementation touchpoints:
- `scripts/check_startup_readiness.py`: runs the two canonical probes, validates required status/watch JSON fields, and emits deterministic failure codes such as `startup_status_probe_failed`, `startup_watch_status_probe_failed`, `startup_watch_payload_invalid`, and `startup_watch_state_contradictory`.
- `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh`: `ensure_startup_readiness()` runs the readiness script after index-freshness checks and fails loud if the probe exits non-zero.
- `scripts/gloggur` and `src/gloggur/bootstrap_launcher.py`: both remediation paths point operators back to `python scripts/check_startup_readiness.py --format json` instead of inventing a separate bootstrap-health command.
- `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md` and `docs/QUICKSTART.md`: publish the same single readiness probe for local worktree sessions.
- `tests/integration/test_bootstrap_env_script.py`, `tests/unit/test_bootstrap_launcher.py`, and `tests/integration/test_bootstrap_wrapper.py`: lock the startup probe, wrapper remediation, and degraded-bootstrap behavior.

The scope boundary is important: this is a startup contract for local Codex/operator sessions. It does not promise that search is warm or that watch mode is enabled; it promises that the repo reports its state honestly and consistently.
acceptance_criteria:
- `python scripts/check_startup_readiness.py --format json` remains the one canonical local worktree readiness probe documented for operators and agents.
- Bootstrap fails loud when either the status probe or the watch-status probe is missing, malformed, or contradictory.
- Wrapper/bootstrap remediation text points back to the same readiness probe instead of a parallel diagnostic path.
- The readiness probe validates machine-readable status/watch contracts and emits deterministic `startup_*` error codes on failure.
- Docs describe startup readiness as an environment-coherence check, not as proof that indexing or watch mode is already complete.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- Direct probe: `./.venv/bin/python scripts/check_startup_readiness.py --format json` returned `ok: true` with `status_probe.needs_reindex=true`, `resume_decision="reindex_required"`, and `watch_probe.status="stopped"` / `running=false` / `pid=null`.
- `tests/integration/test_bootstrap_env_script.py` includes explicit startup-readiness regressions:
  - `test_bootstrap_fails_when_startup_status_probe_fails`
  - `test_bootstrap_fails_when_startup_watch_status_probe_fails`
  - `test_bootstrap_fails_when_startup_watch_payload_is_contradictory`
- `tests/unit/test_bootstrap_launcher.py` and `tests/integration/test_bootstrap_wrapper.py` keep the wrapper/bootstrap remediation and degraded-capability contract aligned with the readiness probe.
- Historical landing context: commit `b28aafa` (`feat: add startup readiness and performance gates; normalize TODO done candidates`) introduced the readiness probe and bootstrap enforcement.

title: [D3] Adopt Beads Task Tracking With Phased Markdown Coexistence
description:
This is the umbrella record for the shipped Beads rollout, not an open-ended “adopt a tracker” placeholder. The concrete repo contract now consists of: Beads-backed net-new task tracking, tracked JSONL export plus fresh-clone bootstrap for `.beads/`, preserved `.githooks` ownership, phased coexistence with `TODOs.md` / `DONEs.md`, and a documented serial-use rule for `bd` commands in this repo. The implementation surface spans `.githooks/pre-commit`, `.githooks/pre-push`, `.githooks/post-merge`, `.githooks/post-checkout`, `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh`, `scripts/check_beads_clone_contract.py`, `.beads/clone-contract.json`, `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, and `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`.

The imported Markdown description still captures the rollout narrative, but the durable value of this issue is the contract it left behind. The finer-grained rollout consequences are now better represented by follow-up records such as `bd-qoe` (panic/serialization), `bd-tip` (Markdown retirement policy), `bd-wee` (setup verification), and `bd-c05` (historical Markdown promotion hygiene).
design:
Current rollout architecture:
- Hook integration remains anchored in `.githooks/`; Beads support is merged into those scripts instead of replacing hook ownership.
- Fresh clones are explicitly `bootstrap_required`: tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl` and `.beads/clone-contract.json` ship in git, while the local Dolt database is rebuilt on demand by `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh`.
- Tracker correctness is checked through `scripts/check_beads_clone_contract.py`, which validates both the tracked contract files and live export parity.
- Agent/operator guidance is split intentionally:
  - `AGENTS.md` for working-session rules
  - `README.md` / `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md` / `docs/QUICKSTART.md` for contributor and automation flows
- Historical Markdown backlog is preserved during coexistence rather than deleted at rollout time.

This umbrella should be treated as the rollout landing record. Future Beads changes should normally land in narrower follow-up issues instead of reopening this umbrella unless they materially change the repo-level tracking model.
acceptance_criteria:
- Net-new work is tracked in Beads and the repo docs say so consistently.
- `.githooks` remains the active hook path, with Beads behavior integrated into the existing hook scripts rather than replacing them.
- Fresh-clone bootstrap from tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl` is documented and enforced through `.beads/clone-contract.json` plus `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh`.
- `.beads/issues.jsonl` stays in parity with the live Beads DB through export-based refresh and clone-contract verification.
- Markdown coexistence, rollback policy, and serialized `bd` usage are documented in the active repo guidance.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- `e95ea06` is the historical landing commit for the repo-wide Beads rollout and hook integration.
- `AGENTS.md` and `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md` now encode the operational rules that matter most after rollout: `bd ready` / `bd update --claim` workflow, Markdown coexistence, clone-contract bootstrap, and serialized `bd` usage.
- `.githooks/pre-commit` currently uses `bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl` and `bd hooks run pre-commit`; `.githooks/pre-push` runs repo tests then `bd hooks run pre-push`; post-merge/post-checkout remain thin Beads shims under `.githooks`.
- `scripts/bootstrap_gloggur_env.sh` and `scripts/check_beads_clone_contract.py` are the concrete code paths that keep the rollout reproducible in fresh clones and guard against tracked/export drift.
- The imported D3 Markdown body still contains rollout-era `bd sync --json` / `bd hook ...` language. The current repo contract has already evolved to `bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl`, `bd hooks run ...`, and clone-contract verification.

title: Clarify subject-repo test and coverage collection environment guidance in docs
description:
This issue was a documentation clarification around an architectural boundary that already exists in the current coverage pipeline: Gloggur imports and ingests coverage artifacts, but it does not run a subject repository's test suite or provision that repository's runner/venv/toolchain. The problem behind the low-signal title was operator confusion about where coverage should be generated during subject-repo analysis.

The current implementation splits the workflow in two stages. First, subject-repo tests and coverage collection must happen in the subject repository's own environment, because that is where its dependency graph, test runner, coverage backend, and executable entrypoints are valid. Second, once a coverage artifact exists, Gloggur can normalize and apply it through `gloggur coverage import ...` and `gloggur coverage ingest ...`. `coverage import` converts source coverage formats into the generic `gloggur-coverage.json` map, and `coverage ingest` then maps executed lines onto indexed symbols inside the active Gloggur cache.

So this issue is best understood as an operator-contract clarification, not a feature change in the coverage engine. The key message that needed to be documented was: do not run subject-repo test/coverage collection inside this Gloggur repo environment and assume the result is meaningful for the analyzed project; instead, produce coverage in the subject repo, then hand the resulting artifact to Gloggur.
design:
Implementation and documentation touchpoints in the current codebase:
- `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`
  - this is the current authoritative operator-facing location for the subject-repo coverage note.
  - it states that test/coverage collection belongs in the subject repo's own venv/runner and that Gloggur should consume the resulting artifact afterward.
- `src/gloggur/cli/main.py`
  - `coverage import-python` and generic `coverage import` are file-to-file conversion commands; they read an existing coverage file and write normalized `gloggur-coverage.json` output.
  - `coverage ingest` reads a normalized JSON coverage mapping and applies it onto indexed symbol metadata; it does not execute tests or collect coverage itself.
- `src/gloggur/coverage_importers.py`
  - the adapter layer reinforces the contract that Gloggur is an importer of produced artifacts, not a test runner.
  - built-in importers currently normalize Python coverage.py SQLite databases and generic JSON context maps.
- `src/gloggur/parsers/coverage.py`
  - `CoverageIngester` applies already-produced line execution maps onto symbols from the metadata store.
- Historical docs landing:
  - commit `3ae5ff1` added the clarification to both `README.md` and `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`.
  - in the current repo state, the surviving authoritative note is in `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`; the README no longer carries that exact sentence after later docs churn.

Recommended contract framing for this issue:
- keep environment ownership explicit: subject repo runs tests, Gloggur consumes artifacts;
- keep path semantics explicit: run `gloggur coverage import ...` from the subject repo cwd or pass explicit artifact paths;
- avoid wording that implies the Gloggur repo's own venv is a generic execution environment for arbitrary analyzed repositories.
acceptance_criteria:
- Operator-facing docs state clearly that subject-repo test and coverage collection must run in the subject repo's own environment/toolchain rather than in this Gloggur repo environment.
- The docs explain the handoff step after coverage is produced: run `gloggur coverage import ...` from the subject repo cwd or provide explicit artifact paths.
- The written guidance matches the current CLI architecture: `coverage import` consumes an existing coverage file, and `coverage ingest` applies normalized coverage data to indexed symbols.
- The docs do not imply that Gloggur itself provisions or runs the subject repo's tests.
- Verification evidence for the note is easy to reproduce from the current repo through doc grep and focused coverage-import CLI regression tests.
notes:
Current verification evidence gathered 2026-03-26:
- Current operator-facing doc location:
  - `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md:233-235` contains the explicit subject-repo coverage note.
  - the exact current guidance is:
    - run subject-repo test/coverage collection in the subject repo's own environment/toolchain;
    - then run `gloggur coverage import ...` from the subject repo cwd or pass explicit artifact paths.
- Current CLI/implementation boundary:
  - `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:9430-9477` defines `coverage ingest`, which reads normalized JSON and updates indexed symbol metadata.
  - `src/gloggur/cli/main.py:9480-9565` defines `coverage import-python` and `coverage import`, which transform already-existing coverage files into Gloggur's normalized artifact format.
  - `src/gloggur/coverage_importers.py` only implements artifact importers (`python`, `python-coverage-sqlite`, `json`); there is no runner/orchestration layer for executing subject-repo tests.
  - `src/gloggur/parsers/coverage.py` applies line maps from a JSON artifact to symbols already present in metadata storage.
- Historical landing context:
  - commit `3ae5ff1` (`docs: clarify subject-repo environment for test and coverage collection`) touched `.beads/issues.jsonl`, `README.md`, and `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`.
  - the original landing added the clarification in both README and the agent guide.
  - in the current repo state, `README.md` no longer contains that exact note, so `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md` is the surviving authoritative location.
- Focused verification on 2026-03-26:
  - `rg -n "subject repo|own environment|coverage import" README.md docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`
  - result: current subject-repo guidance is present in `docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md`.
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q -n0 tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_coverage_import_python_missing_line_data_returns_coverage_sqlite_invalid tests/unit/test_cli_main.py::test_coverage_import_json_adapter_round_trips_context_map`
  - result: `2 passed in 2.19s`
- Gap between the original title and the real work:
  - this was not generic “docs polish”
  - it documented an important boundary in the coverage workflow so operators do not confuse artifact import/ingest with subject-repo test execution responsibility
- Residual doc-contract gap:
  - there is currently no dedicated public-docs test that asserts this exact subject-repo coverage note remains present; verification is currently grep-based plus CLI-surface tests.
close_reason: Completed docs clarification for subject repo test/coverage collection in own environment. Verification: rg -n 'subject repo|own environment|test/coverage collection' README.md docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md. Evidence: README.md, docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md.
